items.title: Pharmaniaga bags RM281.7mil contract from Health Ministry to supply human insulin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pharmaniaga Bhd, through wholly-owned subsidiary Pharmaniaga LifeScience Sdn Bhd (PLS), has secured a three-year contract valued at RM281.7 million from the Ministry of Health for the supply of human insulin to healthcare facilities nationwide. In a statement, the pharmaceutical company said the award reflects the group's established manufacturing capabilities, proven execution track record and continued alignment with national healthcare priorities. Pharmaniaga said it also strengthens the group's recurring revenue base within the public healthcare segment, while further enhancing visibility of earnings over the contract period.

items.title: Bersatu set to work with PH again? Lies, says Radzi Jidin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) vice-president, Datuk Dr Radzi Jidin, has denied claims that a return to cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH) is on the cards. "There is a malicious lie going around claiming that Bersatu will work again with PH. Previous reports had hinted at Bersatu's return to cooperation with PH, following the former's ongoing tensions with Pas.

items.title: IOI Properties Q3 net profit jumps more than threefold to RM258.11mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IOI Properties Group Bhd's net profit jumped 3.39 times year-on-year to RM258.11 million in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, for the current financial year (Q3 FY26) from RM76.13 million in the same period last year. Revenue also surged to RM1.05 billion from RM755.16 million previously. For the nine-month period ended March 31, 2026, IOI Properties said its net profit soared to RM1.63 billion from RM240.08 million previously, while revenue surged to RM3.06 billion from RM2.17 billion previously.

items.title: Putrajaya and Selangor must act fast to ‘lock in’ proposed PJ public hospital, says Kampung Tunku rep
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kampung Tunku assemblyman Lim Yi Wei today urged Putrajaya and the Selangor government to move quickly on plans for a new public hospital in Petaling Jaya, warning that delays in land matters and funding could derail the project. Welcoming the recent announcement of a finalised site for the proposed hospital, Lim said the land should be immediately rezoned as a hospital site under the “Institutional and Public Facilities Zone”. Lim also warned that building a new hospital alone would not resolve deeper structural problems in the healthcare system.

items.title: BCorp's net loss widens to RM176.23mil in Q3 FY26
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Corp Bhd's (BCorp) net loss widened to RM176.23 million in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q3), compared with a net loss of RM92.34 million in the same period a year ago. The group said the higher net loss and lower revenue was mainly due to the performance and contributions from the services and non-food retail business segments. "The lower revenue reported by the non-food retail business was mainly attributed to lower sales contribution from HR Owen Plc, arising from lower sales volume in both the new and used car segments.

items.title: Forex losses, highway provision drag IJM to RM173.89mil net loss in Q4
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Corp Bhd recorded a net loss of RM173.89 million for its fourth quarter ended March 2026 (Q4 FY26), compared to a net profit of RM128.95 million posted in the same period a year earlier, due to foreign exchange losses. In Q4 FY26, IJM recorded net foreign exchange losses of RM94.7 million and recognised an impairment of RM121.6 million on unsold inventories, including an office building in Changchun, China. For the full financial year, IJM posted a lower net profit of RM3.24 million compared with RM403.37 million in the previous financial year, despite a 10 per cent increase in revenue to RM6.87 billion from RM6.25 billion.

items.title: QL Resources posts lower net profit of RM450.35mil in FY26
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: QL Resources Bhd posted a lower net profit of RM450.35 million for the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26), compared with RM455.08 million in the previous financial year. Revenue was almost unchanged at RM7.047 billion, the integrated agro-based business group said in a Bursa Malaysia filing today. For the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2026, QL Resources recorded a higher net profit of RM113.34 million versus RM93.40 million in the corresponding quarter last year.

items.title: Uribe's protege Valencia seeks to become Colombia's first female president
items.summary: Valencia, 48, a scion of two powerful conservative families, is backed by former President Alvaro Uribe, who secured major victories against leftist guerrilla groups in the 2000s, and remains influential despite an ongoing legal case in which he was found guilty of fraud and bribery, convictions later overturned on appeal. Uribe has denied wrongdoing, calling the case a political persecution. Valencia's paternal grandfather was conservative former President Guillermo Leon Valencia, while her maternal grandfather founded a prestigious university.

items.title: Police record statements from seven in search of missing hiker
items.summary: Deputy district police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Fadzil Ismail said the six others were mountain guides involved in the search for Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who has been missing for more than 72 hours and is believed to be without food and water. "We are also sharing information with the Fire and Rescue Department. Also present was Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon.

items.title: Philippine senator charged over flood control scandal
items.summary: MANILA: Philippine prosecutors filed criminal charges on Thursday against a senator allegedly involved in a massive corruption scandal over bogus flood control projects. The charges came almost a year after President Ferdinand Marcos put the so-called "ghost" infrastructure projects, believed to have cost Filipino taxpayers billions of dollars, centre stage during a July speech. On Thursday, Assistant Ombudsman Mico Clavano said the charges against Jose "Jinggoy" Estrada, a senator and son of former Philippine president Joseph Estrada, stemmed from "an intricate mechanism involving illegal budgetary insertions and project allocations" for fiscal year 2025 that allowed him to amass a kickback of over 573 million pesos (US$9.2 million).

items.title: DAP, Umno youth wings trade barbs over coalition loyalty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP and Umno Youth wings have traded barbs over political allegiances, with Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Akmal Saleh saying DAP Youth should not forget that without Barisan Nasional, Pakatan Harapan might not have been able to form the federal government after the last general election. The exchange comes after DAP Youth warned against attempts to undermine the unity government, saying parties within the coalition should remain committed to the mandate of stability and cooperation. Dr Akmal said DAP Youth appeared to have forgotten BN's role in helping PH form the federal government after the last general election.

items.title: GRS: Revised Special Grant rate for Sabah must take effect this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) remains firm on its stance that a revised Special Grant rate for Sabah under Articles 112C and 112D of the Federal Constitution must be implemented this year. In a social media post, Armizan attributed this to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's commitment in the Dewan Rakyat on Nov 13 last year, where the federal government recognised Sabah's 40 per cent Special Grant entitlement as enshrined in the Federal Constitution. Armizan today attended a special discussion session on the implementation status of Sabah's 40 per cent entitlement rights at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC).

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 28, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Thursday as investor sentiment turned increasingly risk-averse following renewed military strikes involving the US and Iran. The ringgit also weakened against the US dollar, closing at 3.9750. Meanwhile, Bitcoin traded lower at RM291,454, while Ethereum also declined to RM7,905.

items.title: Four men with combined 66 criminal offences nabbed after high-speed chase
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Four men with a combined 66 prior criminal offences were arrested after a high-speed chase ended with police intercepting their car at Jalan Air Jernih in Kuala Lumpur early today. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsuddin Mamat said the suspects, all in their 30s, had extensive criminal and drug-related records, with three of them also listed as wanted persons. He said a Mobile Patrol Vehicle (MPV) unit spotted the suspects' vehicle at Persiaran Industri Kota Damansara at 2.23am and instructed the driver to stop for inspection.

items.title: One wild ‘Odyssey’: PJ police nab four suspected thieves after car chase from Kota Damansara to Air Panas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Four local men were arrested after driving dangerously and fleeing from a police patrol car from Kota Damansara to Air Panas here early this morning. Petaling Jaya district police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said the incident began at 2.23am when members of a police patrol car tried to inspect a black Honda Odyssey multi-purpose vehicle (MPV). “The police’s swift action, however, succeeded in intercepting the vehicle at Jalan Air Jernih, Air Panas, Kuala Lumpur before arresting the four suspects,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Ringgit ends lower against greenback as geopolitical concerns persist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Thursday, as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East increased demand for the greenback amid heightened risk aversion. At 6pm, the local currency eased to 3.9670/9770 versus the greenback from Tuesday's close of 3.9660/9705. "The US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.12 per cent to 99.329 points as risk-off sentiment became increasingly prevalent.

items.title: Live snakes, dead bears and brain worms: RFK Jr's wild animal antics
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A viral video of US Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. picking up two writhing black snakes with his bare hands has put his often bizarre adventures with animals back in the spotlight. Kennedy is an unusual government minister, surrounded by controversy over his fringe views that edge into conspiracy theories. And numerous stories have emerged about Kennedy's offbeat antics involving animals, accentuating his eccentric image.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia extends losses amid heightened geopolitical tensions
items.summary: Turnover rose to 3.53 billion units valued at RM4.19 billion compared with 3.24 billion units valued at RM3.72 billion on Tuesday. Warrants turnover slid to 971.39 million units valued at RM117.20 million from 981.14 million units valued at RM133.62 million previously. The ACE Market volume expanded to 583.66 million units valued at RM206.61 million from 471.50 million units valued at RM167.37 million on Tuesday.

items.title: Body of missing 10-year-old boy recovered from Sungai Bernam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 10-year-old boy feared drowned after being swept away by strong currents in Sungai Bernam near Kampung Sungai Nilam in Hulu Selangor was found dead this evening. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Zone 6 chief Mustakim Rimon said the body of Muhamad Aakif Imran Asrul Nizam was recovered at 4.36pm, about 300 metres from the spot where he was believed to have fallen into the river yesterday. Mustakim said the operation involved personnel from the Slim River Fire and Rescue Station, PPDA and the K9 Unit, with assistance from the police, Health Ministry, volunteer fire brigade and the Hulu Selangor District Office.

items.title: Heavy rain, strong winds expected in several states until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for seven states until 9pm today. In the warning issued at 5pm, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather conditions were expected to affect Perak, involving Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar. In Terengganu, the affected areas are Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman, while in Pahang, the warning covers Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera and Rompin.

items.title: Missing Gunung Batu Putih hiker may be searching for water after 72 hours, says Perak Bomba chief
items.summary: TAPAH, May 28 — The condition of female hiker, Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing while climbing Gunung Batu Putih here on May 23, has entered the critical phase after being lost for over 72 hours. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Datuk Sayani Saidon said that in the face of this, the search and rescue operation has been intensified, with more personnel deployed and the search area expanded to areas where the victim may look for food and water. The search will now focus on the area along Sungai Ayer Busok, where the victim may have gone to find water.

items.title: Tan Chong swings into the red in Q1
items.summary: Its revenue for the quarter under review dropped 17.2 per cent to RM457.77 million from RM553 million in the same period last year. Pre-tax earnings dropped 92.1 per cent YoY to RM4.5 million. Additionally, the financial services division recorded revenue of RM14.8 million in Q1 FY26, down RM3.3 million YoY.

items.title: [UPDATED] Teen girl allegedly under influence of drug-laced vape slashes neck, wrist
items.summary: MELAKA: A 14-year-old girl, allegedly under the influence of a drug-laced vape, slashed her neck and wrist after her foster mother stopped her from meeting her boyfriend at a house in Jonker Walk here. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the incident occurred at 10.33pm last Sunday when the teenager became enraged after her 49-year-old foster mother barred her from seeing her 17-year-old boyfriend. "The foster mother claimed the teenager was under severe distress and believed to be under the influence of a 'mushroom' vape before the incident.

items.title: Environmental offences: DoE imposes RM4.59mil in fines, 148 convictions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Environment (DoE) carried out 5,074 inspections nationwide, covering 4,339 premises, between Jan 1 and April 30 this year as part of intensified enforcement under the Environmental Quality Act 1974. In a statement today, the DoE said the nationwide operations also involved inspections of industrial sectors, development projects, motor vehicle emissions, environmental complaints and monitoring to prevent open burning incidents. It said 1,906 enforcement operations were also conducted on motor vehicle emissions involving inspections of 137,722 vehicles nationwide.

items.title: 11,000 pigs remain as Tanjong Sepat farm closures near completion
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT: The closure of pig farms in Tanjong Sepat is in its final phase, with only about 11,000 pigs remaining at farms still operating in stages, the Selangor government said today. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the closure involved about 30 farms following eviction orders issued earlier this year. He said the assistance aimed to help operators transition into other suitable agricultural activities once livestock operations cease completely.

items.title: Press Metal's earnings jumps 35pct in Q1, declares 2.5 sen dividend
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd's saw a 35.2 per cent jump in earnings to RM624.5 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 from RM461.77 million a year ago. Revenue for the quarter under review rose 5.2 per cent to RM4.1 billion from RM3.9 billion last year. The company said that the improved performance was driven mainly by higher metal prices and increased sales volume during the quarter.

items.title: After hot day, MetMalaysia forecasts stormy night across central and southern peninsula, Sabah and Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Thunderstorms are forecast in parts of Perak, Terengganu, Pahang, Negeri Sembilan, Johor, Sarawak and Sabah until 9pm, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said this afternoon. Affected areas in Perak include Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar. In Terengganu, the warning covers Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman.

items.title: YTL Corp Q3 revenue rises to RM7.57bil, profit weighed by weaker power generation
items.summary: Revenue rose to RM7.57 billion from RM7.32 billion a year earlier, while pre-tax profit fell to RM861.6 million from RM987.9 million. Net profit also eased to RM629.2 million from RM744.5 million. YTL Power International Bhd, the group's power arm, recorded higher quarterly revenue of RM5.08 billion versus RM4.9 billion previously, but pre-tax profit dropped to RM442.7 million from RM635.2 million, while net profit declined to RM342 million from RM508.2 million.

items.title: DAP Youth warns against attempts to destabilise unity government
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP Youth has warned against attempts to undermine the unity government, saying parties within the coalition should remain committed to the mandate of stability and cooperation. DAP Youth questioned what it described as efforts by certain quarters within Umno to escalate political tensions, particularly in Johor, despite holding senior positions in the unity government. The youth wing said Pakatan Harapan (PH) had played a significant role in helping Barisan Nasional (BN) remain politically relevant following the coalition's poor performance in the last general election and the 2023 state polls.

items.title: Sombre Aidiladha for family of injured Gaza activist Razman warded in Turkiye
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: It was a sombre Hari Raya Aidiladha for the family of Gaza aid mission volunteer and activist Razman Mat Ali, who is currently receiving medical treatment in Istanbul, Turkiye. "My four children, aged four to 10, understood and supported their father's humanitarian mission. Razman, who suffered a punctured lung after allegedly being assaulted by Israeli soldiers during the humanitarian mission to Gaza, remains under close medical observation.

items.title: Man sentenced to death for brutal abuse and murder of child
items.summary: "The evidence showed that the child was mercilessly assaulted by the accused without compassion. The child, being unable to defend himself, could only cry in pain," she said. The mother could only hear her child's cries from outside a locked room before finding him lifeless," she said.

items.title: Terrain constraints prevent K9 deployment in search for missing hiker
items.summary: TAPAH: The Fire and Rescue Department has not deployed its K9 unit in the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, at Gunung Batu Putih due to the dangerous terrain and vertical rescue limitations. Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said deploying tracking dogs into the densely forested area would require them to be lowered by helicopter winching, posing significant operational risks. Sayani said the SAR operation is now focused on Sungai Air Busuk, as rescuers believe the victim may head toward the river for water since her emergency rations are likely depleted.

items.title: UN condemns 'dangerous escalation' in Ukraine war
items.summary: GENEVA: The United Nations rights chief warned Thursday against a "dangerous escalation" in Ukraine, and Russia's threats to ramp up attacks, urging both sides to return to the negotiating table. Resume negotiations and end the suffering," Volker Turk said in a statement. Eighteen of those killed were women, it said, adding that attacks by Ukrainian armed forces had also killed and injured civilians within Russia itself.

items.title: Hassan: PKR 'injured and wounded', risks collapse if not treated
items.summary: Hassan said he was now only a backbencher on PKR's fringes, choosing silence as he alleged many senior PKR lawmakers had lost confidence in the party while only a handful still firmly backed party president and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. "I see PKR now as injured, wounded and severely hurt. Hassan said he still hoped PKR would survive the alleged internal rebellion.

items.title: Closure of 30 pig farms in Tanjong Sepat enters final phase, with 11,000 animals remaining
items.summary: KUALA LANGAT, May 28 — The closing down of pig farms in Tanjong Sepat, here is in the final phase with only 11,000 remaining animals involved and is expected to be fully completed soon. Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Exco Datuk Izham Hashim said the closure involves about 30 farms that are still operating in various stages, after the eviction order was issued due to the remaining livestock not being mature enough to be disposed of since February. Earlier, Tanjong Sepat assemblyman Datuk Borhan Aman Shah was reported to have said that all pig farms in Tanjong Sepat would be completely closed by the end of June before the enforcement phase is implemented starting July 1.

items.title: LRT users call for better track maintenance after Chan Sow Lin derailment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Users of the Light Rail Transit (LRT) service on the Ampang/Sri Petaling line want maintenance and repair works on the track system to be improved after a train derailed near the Chan Sow Lin Station this morning. A Bernama observation also found that repair work was being carried out by Rapid KL at the incident location, about a three-minute drive from the Chan Sow Lin Station towards Cheras. It was also found that many users still use the train service, which now operates as a shuttle service between Pudu, Chan Sow Lin and Cheras stations.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected in eight states, KL until 6pm today, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Eight states and Kuala Lumpur are expected to experience thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds until 6pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). MetMalaysia, in a warning issued at 3.10pm, stated that the weather conditions are forecast to hit Bandar Baharu in Kedah, while in Perak, it involves Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, and Hilir Perak. The same warning was also issued for Petaling in Selangor, while in Negeri Sembilan it involves Seremban, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin, alongside Segamat in Johor.

items.title: No ‘business-as-usual’: Transport minister orders maximum action against Prasarana after LRT derailment near Chan Sow Lin (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today ordered the “maximum” punitive action against Prasarana Malaysia Bhd following the derailment of an LRT train near Chan Sow Lin station, warning that no member of the rail operator’s top management would be spared accountability over the incident. “I have also directed the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) to take the maximum punitive action against Prasarana Malaysia Bhd over this incident,” he said in a statement today. Prasarana Malaysia Bhd is the state-owned public transport operator that runs the Klang Valley’s LRT, MRT and Monorail rail networks, as well as Rapid KL bus services.

items.title: Rapid KL deploys extra staff, keeps free buses and alternative trains running as Ampang-Sri Petaling repair works continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Rapid KL said alternative train services and free shuttle buses will remain active until the end of today following the derailment near Chan Sow Lin station that disrupted services on the Ampang–Sri Petaling Line earlier this morning. “Repair works are still actively ongoing,” Rapid KL said, adding that “alternative train services and shuttle buses remain active until the end of operating hours”. It also activated several shuttle train services between affected stations, including Bandar Tasik Selatan and Cheras, Ampang and Maluri, as well as Maluri and Chan Sow Lin.

items.title: GOF raids in Kelantan uncover drugs, undocumented migrants and RM1.1m timber haul
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 28 — The General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade carried out four separate operations involving drugs, illegal immigrants, ketum juice and illegal logging activities in Kelantan, with total seizures amounting to RM1.93 million. Its commander, SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain, said the operations were conducted under “Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan” (OTWK) and “Op Bersepadu Khazanah” (OBK), involving cooperation with various enforcement agencies. Ahmad Radzi said in the second case, 19 Myanmar nationals, comprising 13 men and six women aged between 17 and 40, were detained near Kampung Jeram Perdah, Pasir Mas on May 20.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan DKU says no special meeting on May 29 as matter now before courts
items.summary: In the letter dated May 26, DKU secretary Raja Norazli Raja Nordin said the proposed special meeting requested by several Negeri Sembilan royal and customary leaders could not be convened due to ongoing court proceedings. “DKU is unable to convene a special meeting as requested in view of the ongoing court proceedings,” he wrote. Raja Norazli also said the individuals involved could not independently convene or call for a DKU meeting, adding that any purported special meeting scheduled for May 29 would not be recognised as an official DKU meeting.

items.title: Delivery man pleads not guilty to threatening salon assistant in Sentul
items.summary: A. Vinodvarma, 36, is accused of committing the act against Ashha Begum, 24, at Taman Bamboo, Off Jalan Ipoh, Sentul, at 6.20pm on May 22. Earlier, deputy public prosecutor Amritpreet Kaur Randhawa did not offer bail to the accused, as the offence falls under the non-bailable category. However, Vinodvarma’s lawyer, Erickka Farrise Amir, sought bail, saying that her client is the family’s breadwinner and poses no flight risk.

items.title: Thirteen-year-old girl allegedly beaten by group of students after ‘misunderstanding’, say Kota Kinabalu police
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kota Kinabalu police are investigating the alleged assault of a 13-year-old girl who was reportedly beaten by five female students in an incident that surfaced after a video went viral on social media. Police said they received a report from the victim at about 7.51am on May 23, a day after the incident allegedly took place in the city. Initial investigations found the girl was allegedly assaulted by five students from several different schools in the Kota Kinabalu area following what police described as a misunderstanding and lingering dissatisfaction among those involved.

items.title: Submissions for phase four of Bantuan Kasih Johor extended until June 28, says MB
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 28 — The Johor state government has extended the period for the submission of Bantuan Kasih Johor (BKJ) Fourth Phase until June 28 to give more time to recipients to obtain the assistance. “At least 35 review counters and 35 payment counters will be provided for locations with more than 3,000 recipients, while locations with 2,000 recipients and below will involve 10 review counters and 10 payment counters,” he said in a statement after chairing the Fourth Phase BKJ Coordination Meeting here. Onn Hafiz said the operating hours would also be extended to 12 hours, especially in the Johor Bahru district, to reduce congestion and provide comfort to recipients, especially the elderly.

items.title: Nine hurt, including suspected migrant smuggler, after GOF car chase ends in crash near Jeli
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Nine men were injured, three of them seriously, after a car believed to be transporting undocumented migrants crashed near Kampung Sungai Rual in Jeli, Kelantan, this morning. Southeast Brigade General Operations Force commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the remaining victims suffered minor injuries and were treated at Jeli Hospital. He said a General Operations Force (GOF) team from the Seventh Battalion had spotted a suspicious Proton Saga on the East-West Highway.

items.title: ‘Does not tolerate disrespect’: FamilyMart Malaysia suspends employee over viral soup-throwing row with delivery rider
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — FamilyMart Malaysia has suspended an employee accused of throwing soup at a delivery rider following a dispute captured in a viral social media video, according to FMT. “Our team is investigating the incident with urgency and the staff (allegedly) involved has been placed off duty,” FamilyMart Malaysia said in a statement to the media organisation. The incident surfaced after a 23-second video began circulating online today showing a heated exchange between a delivery rider and a FamilyMart employee.

items.title: In Sarawak, three men arrested for suspected trespass into house compound in Tudan Phase 3
items.summary: MIRI, May 28 — Police arrested three local men aged between 21 and 31 on Monday for suspected trespassing into the compound of a house in Tudan Phase 3 here. Miri district police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said police received a report on Sunday night after the homeowner encountered the suspects at his residence at around 9.30pm. Acting on information received, a team from the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of the Miri District Police Headquarters arrested the three men at Kampung Tudan here.

items.title: Malaysian, 22, charged with murder after woman found dead in Singapore HDB lift
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A 22-year-old Malaysian man has been charged with murder after a 21-year-old woman was found dead at a Housing and Development Board (HDB) block in Singapore’s Choa Chu Kang estate earlier this week. According to The Straits Times (ST), Mohamad Faiz Umar was charged in court today over the death of Chua Bee Ting, who was allegedly killed inside a lift at Block 248 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 2 on the night of May 26. Preliminary police investigations indicated that Faiz had allegedly stabbed Chua.

items.title: DoE issues RM4.59m in fines after more than 5,000 inspections in first four months of 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 28 — The Department of Environment (DOE) conducted more than 5,000 inspections nationwide and recorded accumulated fines amounting to RM4.59 million involving environmental pollution cases during the first four months of this year. “Based on the inspections and investigations conducted, the DOE has taken 3,149 follow-up enforcement actions against parties found to be non-compliant with the Environmental Quality Act 1974,” the statement said. In efforts to curb the illegal entry of scheduled waste into the country, DOE said 51 inspection operations were conducted at major ports involving inspections on 702 containers suspected of carrying scheduled waste.

items.title: June rollout of fentanyl test kits signals tougher stance on synthetic drugs, says Nada D-G
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) will begin using specialised testing kits nationwide next month to detect fentanyl and other emerging synthetic drugs, amid mounting concerns over substances being mixed into vape liquids. Ruslin said the kits were capable of detecting fentanyl, synthetic cannabinoids, cocaine and psilocybin — the psychoactive compound found in so-called “magic mushrooms”. “Previously, there were claims that Nada could not test for fentanyl and synthetic substances.

items.title: Man reportedly gored to death by runaway buffalo in Johor, attack video goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — A man was reportedly gored to death by a runaway buffalo in an incident believed to have taken place in the Ulu Tiram area yesterday, with footage of the attack later circulating widely on social media. The victim was later seen getting back to his feet following the attack, although he is understood to have suffered serious injuries. Online , he later died from injuries sustained in the incident.

items.title: Immigration Department says AI-altered passport photos could lead to legal action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The Immigration Department has urged the public not to use photographs generated or altered using artificial intelligence (AI) technology when applying for Malaysian passports. In a Facebook post, the department said the use of AI-generated images or the submission of false information in passport applications was a serious offence that could lead to action under the Passport Act 1966 (Act 150). “Any false photo or information submitted may result in the application being rejected, and the applicant may face action under existing legal provisions,” the department said.

items.title: Missing for three days, Pontian woman, 77, found safe in oil palm plantation
items.summary: Pontian Baru fire and rescue station senior fire officer Mohd Khairul Anwar Ab Ghani said the victim, identified as Nathiah Selamat, was found on the third day of the search and rescue (SAR) operation. The operation commander said the victim was found within a 2.4km radius east of the operation’s incident control post. Mohd Khairul Anwar said the SAR operation involved eight fire and rescue personnel, including the department’s K-9 unit.

items.title: Kelantan police say Malaysia-Thailand border remains secure after Pattani shooting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Police have assured the public that security along the Malaysia-Thailand border in Kelantan remains under control despite a recent armed attack targeting Thai police officers in Pattani. According to Utusan Malaysia , Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said security at border entry points had been reinforced through round-the-clock monitoring and patrols to ensure no threats could compromise national sovereignty. He said police remained on high alert over issues such as illegal immigration, cross-border crime and other potential security threats.

items.title: Anwar donates sacrificial cow to Pahang media practitioners
items.summary: KUANTAN, May 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has donated a sacrificial cow to media practitioners in Pahang for this year’s Aidiladha celebration, in recognition of the role journalists play in connecting the government and the people. Pahang Press chairman Fazli Ahmad said the initiative enabled the distribution of sacrificial meat to journalists and photographers from various print and electronic media organisations, as well as residents of Kampung Anak Air. “Pahang Press would like to express its deepest appreciation to the Prime Minister for donating a cow for the ritual sacrifice, with the meat distributed to media personnel.

items.title: Search for boy, 10, who fell into Sungai Bernam expanded to three-kilometre radius
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 28 — Search and rescue (SAR) operations for a 10-year-old boy who fell into Sungai Bernam in Kampung Sungai Nilam, Gedangsa, Hulu Selangor entered its second day today, with the search area expanded to a three-kilometre radius. According to Berita Harian , Selangor Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the SAR team was still searching for Muhammad Aakif Imran Asrul Nizam as of 11am. “The team is conducting surface searches within a three-kilometre radius from the Fire Control Post (PKB), assisted by other agencies including the Slim River Fire and Rescue Station.

items.title: Body of unidentified man found floating in Sungai Kerayong near Jalan Cheras, believed to be drowned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The body of a man believed to have drowned was found floating in Sungai Kerayong along Jalan Cheras here on Wednesday. Malay daily Sinar Harian reported Cheras district police chief ACP Mohd Rosdi Daud as saying police received information on the discovery of the unidentified male body at about 5.13pm. Mohd Rosdi said a physical examination by the Kuala Lumpur police forensic team found no signs of foul play on the body.

items.title: Stats Dept: 81pc employers report disabled workers perform on par with others
items.summary: National news agency Bernama reported that the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said in its 2025 Employment Survey of Persons with Disabilities that 81 per cent of employers rated PWD workers’ performance on par with other workers, while 71.9 per cent said they contribute to productivity and innovation in the workplace. Nearly 22 per cent of employers currently employ PWD workers. Among current employers, 83.7 per cent employ between one and five PWD workers, with elementary occupations making up 31.4 per cent of roles.

items.title: Dayak community works to keep Gawai heritage alive as traditions fade
items.summary: KUCHING, May 28 — As preparations for Hari Gawai gather pace across Sarawak, concerns are growing over the gradual fading of traditional Dayak customs and rituals amid changing lifestyles and modern influences. Former Sarawak Customs Council research assistant Philip Igai said today’s Gawai celebrations differ greatly from those held between the 1960s and 1980s, when rituals and traditional practices played a much bigger role in the festivities. “In the past, there were more traditional elements such as Gawai Umai and Gawai Tuah, which involved elders and various customary rituals.

items.title: Warisan warns against importing ‘divisive’ religious policies into Sabah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 28 — Warisan has warned against importing what it describes as divisive religious policies from Peninsular Malaysia into Sabah, following controversy over Selangor planning guidelines restricting non-Muslim places of worship from operating in commercial areas and shoplots. Warisan vice-president Datuk Junz Wong said Sabah has had a long-standing culture of moderation, religious tolerance and peaceful coexistence that must not be undermined by policies that risk creating unease among minority communities. “Sabahans deserve to know where Sabah DAP stands on this matter,” he said in a statement, Wednesday.
